skill.name: ki-register-use-case-documenter
skill.description: Document AI use cases for EU AI Act governance, inspect code or interview users, then validate and submit confirmed manifests to KI-Register.
skill.summary: Creates structured AI use case manifests for EU AI Act governance, with code inspection, stakeholder interviews, and validated submission to KI-Register.
skill.author: Egonso

security_audit.summary: All 45 static findings are false positives. The backtick patterns are Markdown inline code formatting, not shell execution. Hardcoded URLs point to the legitimate KI-Register service. Path traversal sequences are relative path documentation. API key references are environment variable names and example placeholders, not real secrets. The skill follows proper security practices: secrets via environment variables, explicit human confirmation before submission, and no hardcoded credentials.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 26 instances of backtick characters as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are all Markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `studio-agent capture`, `docs/agent-workflows/<slug>/manifest.json`) used in documentation to reference CLI commands and file paths. No actual shell execution occurs from these patterns.

low_findings.title: Relative path references in documentation
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged path traversal sequences in line 109. These are relative path references in documentation (../../schemas/, ../../examples/) showing where schema and example files are located relative to the skill. No file system access is performed by these patterns.

content.user_title: Document AI Use Cases for EU AI Act Compliance
content.value_statement: Organizations deploying AI systems need structured governance records for EU AI Act compliance, but creating them from codebases and stakeholder knowledge is time-consuming. This skill inspects code or interviews users to produce validated, reviewable manifests ready for KI-Register submission.
actual_capabilities: Inspects local codebases to extract AI use case evidence from READMEs, routes, configs, and prompts
Conducts structured stakeholder interviews covering purpose, data, controls, and human checkpoints
Generates machine-readable manifest.json files in docs/agent-workflows/<slug>/
Creates human-readable README.md review surfaces for legal, compliance, and engineering teams
Validates manifests locally before submission to catch errors early
Submits confirmed manifests to the KI-Register API with explicit user authorization
limitations: Requires a KI-Register account and API key (KI_REGISTER_API_KEY) for submission
Provides governance documentation support, not legal advice or conformity assessments
Cannot classify AI risk levels definitively; uncertain classifications must be reviewed by humans
Depends on the studio-agent CLI being available in the working repository

use_cases.title: Compliance Officer Building Audit Records
use_cases.description: A compliance officer needs to document all AI systems in use across the organization for an upcoming EU AI Act audit. The skill inspects each project repository, extracts evidence of AI usage, and produces standardized manifests ready for review.

use_cases.title: Engineering Lead Documenting a New AI Feature
use_cases.description: An engineering team has just shipped a feature that uses an LLM for content generation. Before launch, the lead uses the skill to interview the team, capture the workflow, data categories, and human oversight mechanisms, and submit the manifest to KI-Register.

use_cases.title: Procurement Team Evaluating AI Vendors
use_cases.description: A procurement specialist receives an AI vendor proposal and needs a structured use case record to evaluate against internal governance requirements. The skill captures the vendor's workflow details and produces a comparable manifest.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Codebase Inspection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ki-register-use-case-documenter to inspect this project. Read the README, main entry points, and configuration files. Produce a draft manifest covering the AI use case, data categories, and human checkpoints. Mark anything unclear as missing.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time documentation of an existing AI project

prompt_templates.title: Stakeholder Interview Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ki-register-use-case-documenter in interview mode. Ask me one question at a time to document this AI workflow. Start with the intended purpose, then cover data, decision influence, human oversight, risks, and controls.
prompt_templates.scenario: Documenting a process not visible in code

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Submit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ki-register-use-case-documenter to validate the manifest at docs/agent-workflows/<slug>/manifest.json. If it passes, show me the summary and wait for my explicit confirmation before submitting to KI-Register.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Onboarding
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ki-register-use-case-documenter to onboard all AI-related projects in the ./services directory. For each project, run capture, validate, and queue the manifests for my review before any submission.

output_examples.input: Document the AI use case in this project. Start by reading the README and main configuration.
output: Identified AI component: content-moderation service using an LLM classifier
Data categories: user-generated text, moderation labels
Human checkpoint: human reviewer for flagged content above confidence threshold
README generated with purpose, systems, risks, and controls
3 fields marked as missing — requires follow-up interview

output_examples.input: Validate and submit the manifest for the customer-support-chatbot use case.
output: Validation: passed (all required fields present, schema compliant)
Summary: AI-assisted chatbot for tier-1 support, 12 systems, 4 human checkpoints
Awaiting explicit confirmation before submission to KI-Register

best_practices: Always validate the manifest locally before requesting submission approval
Never write API keys into generated manifest files, READMEs, or logs
Use codebase inspection first and interview mode only to fill in gaps
Mark uncertain risk classifications as 'needs review' rather than guessing
anti_patterns: Submitting a manifest without explicit human confirmation of the summary
Hardcoding KI_REGISTER_API_KEY in scripts or committing it to version control
Overclaiming risk classification without supporting evidence in the codebase
Creating duplicate workflow folders for the same operational use case

faq.question: What is KI-Register?
faq.answer: KI-Register is a governance platform for EU AI Act compliance. It stores structured AI use case records for audit preparation, procurement, and regulatory review.

faq.question: Do I need a KI-Register account to use this skill?
faq.answer: You can generate and validate manifests without an account. An account and API key are only required for the final submission step.

faq.question: How does the skill handle my API key?
faq.answer: The skill expects the API key in the KI_REGISTER_API_KEY environment variable. It explicitly avoids writing keys into manifests, READMEs, logs, or version control.

faq.question: Can it classify my AI system's risk level under the EU AI Act?
faq.answer: No. The skill documents evidence and flags uncertain areas. Risk classification must be done by qualified human reviewers.

faq.question: What capture mode should I use?
faq.answer: Use codebase inspection when the AI is visible in the repository. Use stakeholder interview when the process is organizational. Use low-friction capture when most context is already known.

faq.question: Is the output legally binding?
faq.answer: No. The skill supports structured governance documentation and review. It is not a substitute for legal advice or a conformity assessment.
